prose:
Fukui-descriptor reactivity indices calculated for pyridine using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier standard) in the gas phase with Mulliken partial charges (PySCF backend). The calculation computed three electronic states (neutral, cation, and anion) to derive the Fukui indices f⁺ (electrophilic reactivity), f⁻ (nucleophilic reactivity), and f⁰ (radical reactivity). SCF converged for all states. The most electrophilic site is nitrogen with f⁺ = 0.48423 (highest nucleophilic attack susceptibility); the most nucleophilic site is the para carbon (atom 4) with f⁻ = 0.11826 (highest electrophilic attack susceptibility). Charge-conservation integrity checks passed: Σf⁺ = 1.000 and Σf⁻ = 1.000, confirming proper convergence. No warnings were reported. The structure contains 11 atoms (1 N + 5 C + 5 H) in a neutral singlet state.</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.48423
